tracks.title: Track A
tracks.counterexample: A reader may infer a general recursive-improvement capability from bounded causal-chain evidence.
tracks.safe_boundary: Bounded causal-chain evidence supports only exact public-safe evidence chains, not general recursive-improvement capability.

tracks.title: Track B
tracks.counterexample: A reader may infer hidden prompt or instruction mutation is allowed.
tracks.safe_boundary: The evidence excludes hidden instruction mutation and records no authority for prompt, instruction, or policy changes.

tracks.title: Track C
tracks.counterexample: A reader may infer AO can change policy or authority boundaries autonomously.
tracks.safe_boundary: The evidence preserves policy-changing autonomy denial and requires separate gates for authority changes.

tracks.title: Track D
tracks.counterexample: A reader may treat bounded causal-chain generality as broad_RSI approval.
tracks.safe_boundary: The evidence keeps broad_RSI denied and approves only counterexample-tested bounded causal-chain wording.

approved_safe_boundary: bounded causal-chain evidence with counterexample-tested claim boundaries
